articleBody: Photographers Copyright - digital images and the internet PHOTOGRAPHERS COPYRIGHTCopyright notice: digital images, photographs and the internetPhotographers Copyright Guidance I get asked the question on copyright on numerous occasions by clients so thought I'd this with the community. It covers pretty much everything so worth reading and implementing a copyright notice on your website if you have one or at least knowing the legal position if you find your images being copyright infringed. There's a lot of misinformation, myth and even paranoia regarding photo copyright so hopefully this will dispel and clarify some key points for you. Copyright notice: digital images, photographs and the internet There are several websites that now provide a scanning service to discover if your images have been used, without your permission. It may take some time for them to scan and match infringements and generally they are only interested in a legal case on your behalf if the infringement for commercial or business use. If they find commercial infringement they will take the case on your behalf and deduct a service fee from any royalty/usage fee they receive from the offender. Official UK copyright guidance Copyright guidance for digital images and photographs For official UK guidance on copyright, digital images, photographs and online use, see the Intellectual Property Office’s current GOV.UK guidance page. The earlier PDF version has been replaced by an accessible HTML page, so it is better to link directly to the GOV.UK source rather than re-hosting an older PDF. Recommended source: Copyright notice: digital images, photographs and the internet — UK Intellectual Property Office.
